top_signals.reason: Boeing holds two NASA contracts totaling over $32.9B triggering the >$1B rule twice (+30pts combined) plus cross-reference match with 31 corruption indicators and social mentions amplifying via 2+ indicator cross-reference bonus (+25pts). Scale and agency concentration create a strong institutional tailwind for BA.

Hyatt Hotels shows the highest dark pool volume and trade count in the dataset suggesting significant institutional accumulation away from lit markets. No political or contract signal to cross-reference but volume concentration warrants attention.

Bechtel National holds a $17.2B Department of Energy contract exceeding $1B threshold (+15pts) but zero social mentions and zero corruption indicators limit cross-reference amplification. Privately held entity reduces direct equity play.

Offshore confirmed match at 100% adds +30pts but no corroborating congressional trade, contract, or market-moving signal exists to cross-reference. Insufficient actionable signals to recommend a position.
Paradise Papers confirmed match at 100% scores +30pts but no legislative, contract, or trade signal cross-references this entity. Score too isolated to justify a trade action.
Confirmed offshore match at 100% scores +30pts but Cohen holds no current market-relevant office or contract-influencing position. No actionable cross-reference available.

Boozman purchased IEF and IEI on the same date suggesting a deliberate flight-to-safety rotation into Treasuries; multiple same-ticker-cluster signals apply x1.3 multiplier on the pair. Possible insider awareness of rate or macro policy shift.

Same-day Treasury bond ETF purchase alongside IEF by Boozman creates a coordinated defensive signal warranting x1.3 multiplier. Concurrent rotation out of growth equities reinforces macro caution thesis.
Bruker Corp shows moderate dark pool activity with no corroborating political or contract signals to elevate conviction. Standalone dark pool signal only.
Senate sale of eToro at notable size triggers congressional trade signal. No cross-reference indicators or additional signals to amplify score.
Congressional purchase of First Citizens Bancorporation with no additional amplifying signals. Small position size limits conviction.
Congressional purchase of JPMorgan Chase with no cross-reference or contract signals present. Minimal size reduces signal strength.
Senate purchase of PepsiCo at moderate size with no amplifying signals. Likely defensive positioning with no insider catalyst identified.
Sale of S&P 500 Growth ETF with no additional amplifying signals present. Standalone congressional trade signal only.
Congressional purchase of Eaton Corp with no contract or cross-reference amplifiers present. Small size limits actionability.

Trump sued his own IRS.

Settled with his own government.

Secured a $1.8 billion slush fund to siphon toward his allies.

Blocked the IRS audits against himself and his family. 

This is corruption i
